Add comprehensive compatibility test suite for system libraries

This commit adds a new test file (dl/compat_test.go) that implements the
compatibility testing strategy described in ROADMAP.md Phase 5.

Test Coverage:
- TestCompatibility_CustomLibrary: Baseline test with known-working library
- TestCompatibility_libm: Math library (M4 checkpoint test)
- TestCompatibility_libz: Compression library
- TestCompatibility_libc: C standard library with symbol versioning
- TestCompatibility_MultipleSystemLibraries: Concurrent library loading
- TestCompatibility_DependencyChain: Transitive dependency verification
- TestCompatibility_RTLD_GLOBAL: Global symbol visibility
- TestCompatibility_SymbolVersioning: GNU symbol versioning
- BenchmarkCompatibility_libm_cos: Performance baseline

Status:
- Custom library tests: PASS ✓
- System library tests: SKIP by default (requires PURE_GO_DL_TEST_SYSTEM_LIBS=1)

Known Issue:
System libraries (libm, libz, libc) currently crash during initialization
with SIGSEGV when their init functions execute. This appears to be an issue
with IFUNC resolution or other complex glibc init mechanisms. The tests are
implemented but skipped by default to prevent CI failures.

The tests can be enabled with: PURE_GO_DL_TEST_SYSTEM_LIBS=1

This establishes the testing framework for system library compatibility as
described in ROADMAP.md, even though full system library support still needs
work on the init function handling.

Statistics:
- New file: dl/compat_test.go (495 lines)
- Test functions: 8 tests + 1 benchmark
- Zero complexity regression (go-stats-generator diff: stable)
- All existing tests continue to pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Watch symbols (#164)

This pull request introduces a new Watchlist API to the project,
enabling CRUD operations for user watchlists, and adds a comprehensive
set of models and endpoints for education-related features in the
Intelligence and Recommendation APIs. It also extends the configuration
and data models to support these new features.

**Watchlist API Implementation:**

- Added a new `WatchlistRepository` for managing watchlist data in
`watchlist.json`, including methods to list, upsert, and delete
watchlist items.
- Introduced new Pydantic models in `api/models/watchlist.py` for
watchlist items, upsert requests, and responses.
- Created a new FastAPI router `api/routers/watchlist.py` with endpoints
to list, add/update, and delete watchlist items.
- Registered the new watchlist router in the main API, updated
dependencies to provide the repository, and ensured the watchlist file
path is managed.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-e82360825567810a0ff980e514645a2494c80fc525f0c77d59a61bb2b30437c3R16)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-e82360825567810a0ff980e514645a2494c80fc525f0c77d59a61bb2b30437c3R30)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-e82360825567810a0ff980e514645a2494c80fc525f0c77d59a61bb2b30437c3R47-R51)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-e82360825567810a0ff980e514645a2494c80fc525f0c77d59a61bb2b30437c3R60-R63)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-c2c3b0c64a2690193a471cfc8068fcb622a23e1b4323c693b6ba20835f26fb88R23)
[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-c2c3b0c64a2690193a471cfc8068fcb622a23e1b4323c693b6ba20835f26fb88R260)

**Intelligence Education Feature:**

- Added models for education generation requests and responses,
including error handling and output structure, to
`api/models/intelligence.py`.
- Extended the Intelligence router with endpoints to generate and
retrieve education content for symbols.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-969a21c364a926bafb609fcf4a97596d381195618a2b14ab5ff35790611e930aR11-R12)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-969a21c364a926bafb609fcf4a97596d381195618a2b14ab5ff35790611e930aR193-R209)
- Updated the intelligence config model to support education prompt
templates, forbidden claim categories, and related configuration.

**Recommendation Education Feature:**

- Added models for generated education views, errors, and payloads in
the Recommendation API.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0966a70cb1a9078bb9690268dfc146b3bd08250f531095e31582c09c38d20168R60-R93)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0966a70cb1a9078bb9690268dfc146b3bd08250f531095e31582c09c38d20168L68-R102)

**Project-wide Model and Import Updates:**

- Updated `api/models/__init__.py` to expose new watchlist and education
models.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-beb3cbd98627983f90284c36d2bd1a7a44fecc6ef7d7f03ee1f8bb249f3f9893R71-R76)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-beb3cbd98627983f90284c36d2bd1a7a44fecc6ef7d7f03ee1f8bb249f3f9893R146-R149)

These changes provide a foundation for user-facing watchlist management
and enhanced educational content generation and retrieval throughout the
API.

blocked until the agent finished. pollUntilDone was never reached,
making all budget/delegation enforcement blind.

Fixes:

* fix(temporal): completion detection requires session idle status

isSessionDone was declaring sessions complete prematurely — it saw
step-finish with no running tools, but the agent was between steps.

AND session status API reporting idle/completed before declaring done.

* feat(governor): per-session budget enforcement for child sessions

pollUntilDone now discovers child sessions each poll cycle via
parentID and enforces budget on EACH session individually:
- Parent: token, cost, and delegation enforcement
- Each child: token and cost enforcement (aborts just that child)
- abortSession cascades: kills children first, then parent
- Completion requires parent done AND all children idle
- Logs show child count and aggregate child cost per poll

task tool ran completely ungoverned ($1.99 and $1.32 on two
children while parent was correctly killed at $0.69).

LLM and produces a compact JSON digest. The parent session receives
the manifest pre-injected into its prompt instead of doing 6+ bash
tool calls to discover the universe.

Bootstrap manifest shape:
- repo: branch, head, dirty
- beads: total, open, closed counts
- targets: id, status, title, description for each open child bead
- invariants: parent contract rules

Workflow changes:
- New 'bootstrapping' phase between session_created and sending_prompt
- Manifest injected as <bootstrap_manifest> XML block in prompt
- When bootstrapped, maxExplorationBeforeDelegation = 0 (parent
  MUST NOT call any exploration tools — it has everything it needs)
- PUNCH: bootstrap_complete records target count, branch, head

CLI flags: --repo-dir, --bd-path, --epic-id

Expected impact: parent drops from 5 turns ($0.83) to ~2 turns (<$0.30)
by eliminating the transcript snowplow from bash discovery.

commit.message:
fix: patch qwtconfig.pri with sed instead of inline qmake -= args

The WASM qmake is a wrapper script that calls the host gcc_64/bin/qmake6.
It does not quote all arguments, so 'QWT_CONFIG -= QwtFramework' gets
split into three tokens and qmake6 sees '-=' as an unknown option (exit 1).

Use sed to delete the unwanted QwtFramework/Svg/OpenGL/Designer/Examples
lines from qwtconfig.pri before invoking qmake, which needs no special
argument quoting and is more portable.

commit.message:
fix: strip :6443 from server URL for Harvester Steve/upload API

Harvester kubeconfigs downloaded from the UI point to the Kubernetes API
on port 6443 (https://<vip>:6443). The image status check and the upload
action (?action=upload) are served by the Harvester Steve API on port 443,
not port 6443. Querying https://<vip>:6443/v1/harvesterhci.io.*/... always
returned a non-200 response, causing _do_upload() to poll for 5 minutes
and time out without ever seeing the Initialized condition.

Fix: strip the ':6443' suffix from the server URL before forming Steve API
paths. Rancher-managed kubeconfigs (server = https://rancher/.../k8s/clusters/
local) have no :6443 suffix and are unaffected.

Also improve the timeout error message to show the last HTTP code and the
exact URL so failures are easier to diagnose in the future.

Applied to both harvester-vm.sh (_do_upload) and image/main.tf (terraform_data).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: eliminate volume feedback loop on MA path

Three independent paths were setting PA sink volume simultaneously:
1. api.py sent set_volume to subprocess (→ bridge_daemon → pactl)
2. MA echoed VolumeChanged back via sendspin protocol (→ bridge_daemon → pactl)
3. ma_monitor received player_updated event (→ direct pactl)

This caused volume to bounce/jump on every change and on track start.

Fix: let bridge_daemon be the single writer to pactl via the sendspin
protocol echo. Remove subprocess command from API MA path and replace
ma_monitor's direct pactl call with a status-only update.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: install WASM host Qt to same dir as wasm_singlethread so qmake wrapper resolves

The wasm_singlethread qmake wrapper script calls the sibling path
Qt/6.6.3/gcc_64/bin/qmake6. When gcc_64 is installed to a separate
dir (qt-host), that path doesn't exist and qmake exits 127.

Remove the dir: override on the host Qt install so both wasm_singlethread
and gcc_64 land under the same default base directory, making the
relative sibling path resolvable.

commit.message:
Fix fsyncDir panic on tmpfs and replicatePut corruption race (#6)

Two fixes for failing tests:

### 1. `fsyncDir` panic on tmpfs

`fsyncDir` panics on tmpfs (i.e. `/tmp`) because Zig's
`std.posix.fsync()` maps `EINVAL` to `unreachable` rather than a
catchable error. This crashes both the unit test for `copyFileWithSync`
and the E2E replication test (process aborts before `.sum` sidecar is
copied).

- Replace `posix.fsync(dir.fd) catch {}` with `_ =
std.posix.system.fsync(dir.fd)` to use the raw syscall directly,
bypassing the `unreachable` trap

```zig
// Before: panics on EINVAL/EBADF/EROFS (unreachable, not catchable)
posix.fsync(dir.fd) catch {};

// After: raw syscall returns int, safely discarded
_ = std.posix.system.fsync(dir.fd);
```

Cross-platform: resolves to raw Linux syscall or C library `fsync()` on
macOS via `std.posix.system`.

### 2. `replicatePut` corruption race condition

When the process is killed between replication completing and log
truncation finishing, stale replication log entries get replayed on
remount. This causes `replicatePut` to copy the corrupted backing file
over the good replica, making the scrub unable to repair (it sees
"replica also corrupt"). This caused the E2E `self_healing` test to fail
intermittently.

- Add a pre-replication integrity check in `replicatePut` that computes
the BLAKE3 hash of the backing file and compares it against the stored
`.sum`. If they don't match, replication is skipped so the scrub can
repair from the still-good replica.
